page: Amazon Web Services m8i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 32 dedicated vCPUs derived from 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. The system includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in text processing and lightweight language model execution, with Geekbench text processing and small-model LLM text generation reaching top-tier levels. Single-threaded and multi-threaded decompression tasks, along with image processing and static web serving throughput, fall into the top 25% tier. Conversely, memory bandwidth for large blocks and standard database operations on Passmark align with the middle 50% average, showing a relative tradeoff between raw computational throughput and memory-bound database transaction speeds.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8i.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads that require modern instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions without the premium cost of dedicated GPU accelerators. This makes the instance appropriate for web serving, software compilation, text processing pipelines, and small-to-medium scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show top-tier results in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong single- and multi-threaded decompression and static web serving capabilities. Standard database operations and large-block memory bandwidth perform within the average range, indicating a tradeoff for memory-intensive workloads. This instance is suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, web serving, and moderate machine learning inference tasks that leverage its built-in Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance in text processing and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix acceleration for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation
Baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps with no local storage or GPU
Supports advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and text processing pipelines
